name: Lifeward Ltd.
business: Lifeward designs, develops, and commercializes medical devices for physical rehabilitation, including robotic exoskeletons for spinal cord injury (ReWalk), anti-gravity treadmills (AlterG), and soft exo-suits for stroke (ReStore). It sells to rehabilitation clinics, hospitals, individuals, and through distributors in the U.S., Europe, and other markets.

exposures.sentence: We are a medical device company that designs, develops, and commercializes life-changing solutions that span the continuum of care in physical rehabilitation and recovery, delivering proven functional and health benefits in clinical settings as well as in the home and community.

These devices are robotic exoskeletons that are designed for individuals with paraplegia that use our patented tilt-sensor technology and an onboard computer and motion sensors to drive motorized legs that power movement.
Our proprietary Stride Smart software can provide real-time data and analytics so that the user can watch and self-correct gait abnormalities.

The AlterG Anti-Gravity systems use patented, National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) derived differential air pressure (DAP) technology to reduce the effects of gravity and allow patients to rehabilitate with finely calibrated support and reduced pain.

These devices are robotic exoskeletons that are designed for individuals with paraplegia that use our patented tilt-sensor technology and an onboard computer and motion sensors to drive motorized legs that power movement.

catalysts.event: FDA 510(k) clearance for ReWalk 7 Personal Exoskeleton
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, we received 510(k) clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the ReWalk 7 Personal Exoskeleton device, a next-generation ReWalk model.

catalysts.event: Finalized agreement with BARMER for reimbursement process
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we finalized an agreement with BARMER to formalize the reimbursement process for the provision of ReWalk exoskeletons to medically eligible beneficiaries.

catalysts.event: CMS revised DMEPOS Fee Schedule to include lump-sum Medicare payment for personal exoskeletons
catalysts.sentence: On April 11, 2024, CMS revised its April 2024 Durable Medical Equipment, Prosthetics, Orthotics, and Supplies (DMEPOS) Fee Schedule to include a final lump-sum Medicare purchase fee schedule amount for personal exoskeletons (HCPCS code K1007) with an established rate of $91,032.

catalysts.event: Administrative Law Judge ruled in favor of Medicare beneficiary for ReWalk coverage
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, an Administrative Law Judge (ALJ) ruled in favor of a Medicare beneficiary's appeal and determined that their ReWalk Personal Exoskeleton shall be covered and reimbursed by Medicare as a reasonable and necessary medical device that enables walking after SCI.

catalysts.event: Entered into Share Purchase Agreement with Oramed to acquire Oratech
catalysts.sentence: On January 12, 2026, we entered into a Share Purchase Agreement with Oramed Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (Oramed) and Oratech Pharma, Inc. (Oratech), pursuant to which we agreed to acquire all of the outstanding equity interests of Oratech, a wholly owned subsidiary of Oramed.

catalysts.event: Entered into Secured Promissory Note with Oramed for bridge financing
catalysts.sentence: On November 14, 2025, we entered into a Secured Promissory Note (the Initial Secured Promissory Note) with Oramed Ltd., pursuant to which we issued to Oramed Ltd. a secured promissory note in the principal amount of $3.0 million.
On March 12, 2026, our shareholders approved the transaction.

catalysts.event: Announced exclusive distribution agreement with CorLife for workers compensation market
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, we announced an agreement with CorLife, LLC., a Delaware limited liability company (CorLife) and a division of Numotion, the nation's leading and largest provider of products and services that provide mobility, health and personal independence, to increase our penetration of SCI Products into the workers compensation market.

catalysts.event: Announced distribution agreement with Verita Neuro for international markets
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we announced a distribution agreement with Verita Neuro, a provider of intensive neurological rehabilitation services.

catalysts.event: Entered into Intellectual Property Assignment and Technology Transfer Agreement with Skelable
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we entered into an Intellectual Property Assignment and Technology Transfer Agreement with Skelable Ltd., an Israeli technology company, pursuant to which we agreed to acquire certain intellectual property and related technology assets associated with a powered upper-body robotic orthotic system designed to assist individuals with impaired upper-limb function, including stroke survivors.

tamClaims.claim: 308,000 people in the U.S. living with SCI, with 18,000 new cases per year
tamClaims.sentence: The NSCISC estimates according to its 2025 SCI Fact Sheet that there are approximately 308,000 people in the United States living with SCI, with an annual incidence of approximately 18,000 new cases per year.

tamClaims.claim: 1,200 certified inpatient rehabilitation facilities in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the MedPAC 2025 Report, there are approximately 1,200 certified inpatient rehabilitation facilities in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: 54,000 outpatient clinics in the U.S. in 2024
tamClaims.sentence: According to the IBIS World Industry Report (which tracks the number of physical therapy rehabilitation centers), there were approximately 54,000 outpatient clinics in the U.S. in 2024.
Globally, we estimate that there are over 100,000 outpatient clinics based on scaling of population and standard of living that there are over 100,000 outpatient clinics.

tamClaims.claim: 1,400 sports programs in the U.S. as potential AlterG customers
tamClaims.sentence: Based on our internal estimates of the market, we believe that there are approximately 1,400 sports programs in the U.S. who are potential AlterG customers.

tamClaims.claim: 800,000 stroke cases per year in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Stroke incidence in the United States is estimated at approximately 800,000 cases per year, with roughly 75–80% of individuals surviving the acute event.

tamClaims.claim: 400,000 annual stroke patients who require walking assistance after discharge
tamClaims.sentence: The ReBoot is a customizable personalized device intended for home and community use with an estimated market of approximately 400,000 annual stroke patients who require walking assistance after being discharged home.

tamClaims.claim: Over 6,000 facilities globally using AlterG systems
tamClaims.sentence: AlterG Anti-Gravity systems are utilized in over 6,000 facilities globally in more than 40 countries.

tamClaims.claim: Paraplegia complications cost approximately $670,000 in the first year post-injury
tamClaims.sentence: The National Spinal Cord Injury Statistical Center (NSCISC) estimates that complications related to paraplegia cost approximately $670,000 in the first-year post-injury, excluding indirect costs such as loss in wages, fringe benefits, and productivity, and significant additional amounts over the course of an individual's lifetime.
